transcript_facts:
* The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
* The patient denies blood in stool.
* Hemoglobin is 8.2.
* The clinician plans an anemia profile.
* The clinician starts Lasix 40 mg daily.
* The patient noticed the spot a month ago.
* The biopsy was 0.7 millimeters.
* The patient's mom and sister had melanoma.
* The patient's grandfather had pancreatic cancer.
* The patient has no health problems.
* The patient is allergic to augmentin.
* The patient owns an auto repair shop.
* The patient smokes from time to time.
* The patient had gall bladder and appendix surgery.
* The clinician observed a slightly abnormal lymph node in the right groin.
* The clinician recommends an ultrasound of the right groin.
* The melanoma was classified as Clark level 4.
* The biopsy site was a shave biopsy site.
* The lesion was superficial spreading.
* The clinician recommends a wide local excision.
* The clinician does not recommend complex surgery.
* The clinician recommends regular follow-up with the dermatologist.
* The clinician recommends sunscreen use.
